fields.description:
Run the ADR-0036-mandated version-segmented threshold replay for the plan-v2 impact model ("calibration 3") and adjudicate current blocking thresholds against it. Calibration 2 (2026-07-08) lowered six criteria 0.70->0.60 adjudicated against the OLD mean impact model; the plan-v2 severity-first MAX model with the 0.85 hard-override floor (src/rebar/llm/review_kernel/decide.py::impact_plan) shipped the same day and has never been replayed. The block-rate rise (~25% pre-cal-2 to 52-67% after) was measured in the d195 calibration analysis and is recorded in the comments of ticket 0730-6ceb-88c3-430c (analysis task) and epic d195's evidence trail - that analysis is the grounded source for the motivation numbers. This ticket produces the evidence base; threshold changes are applied here only if the replay warrants them per the decision rule below. The dependent floor-split story that consumes this ticket's AC4 evidence is large-sleepful-needlefish (7d91-55fc-e07c-4101), already linked depends_on this ticket.

## What
- Corpus pre-check (already performed, recorded here for reproducibility): the sidecar corpus at .tickets-tracker/*/*-REVIEW_RESULT.json holds 2059 sidecars: 1007 tagged impact_model_version=plan-v2, 835 untagged (pre-tagging), 150 code-v3, 67 code-v2. GO/NO-GO floor: proceed iff the plan-v2 segment has >=500 sidecars AND >=5 non-DET criteria reach the script's MIN_N=25 finding floor; otherwise stop and record a low-power decision on this ticket. (The >=500 pre-check already passes; MIN_N coverage is confirmed from the replay output itself.)
- Extend docs/experiments/calibrate_plan_review_thresholds.py so load() counts skipped sidecars by reason (different-version vs untagged) and main() prints the skipped remainder alongside the segment size - the script currently discards non-matching sidecars silently, so AC1's "skipped remainder" needs this counter. Land this script change (plus the calibration record, below) via Gerrit.
- Run `python docs/experiments/calibrate_plan_review_thresholds.py --impact-model-version plan-v2` from the main checkout (where .tickets-tracker lives) and record its full output as a comment on this ticket.
- Adjudicate each non-DET blocking-tier criterion (current tiers: 0.60 for the six cal-2 criteria; 0.70 for T1, T8, G1G2; 0.75 for E4) against the replay proposal using this decision rule: (a) script class LOW-DATA (n<25) -> KEEP current, record low-data; (b) script class FP-PRONE (mean validity <0.45 or P(indeterminate)>0.20) -> DEMOTE to advisory OR raise threshold to >=0.90, choose and record which; (c) script class BLOCK-ELIGIBLE -> adopt max(0.5, p90 rounded to nearest 0.05) when it differs from the current threshold by >=0.05 in either direction (this is the raise path when p90 > current+0.05 and the lower path when p90 < current-0.05), else KEEP; (d) script class ADVISORY-KEEP on a currently-blocking criterion -> KEEP threshold but record the borderline signals for calibration 4. Every decision cites the criterion's replay row (n, validity, rev_rr, p90, fire rate).
- Record the adjudication durably: append a "Calibration 3 (plan-v2 segmented replay)" section to docs/experiments/plan-review-threshold-calibration.md with the replay table, per-criterion decisions, and rationale - landed in the same Gerrit change as the script counter (and any warranted criteria_routing.json threshold edits). The edit target for threshold values is src/rebar/llm/plan_review/criteria_routing.json (NOT src/rebar/llm/criteria/, which holds only the resolver machinery).
- Floor-attribution analysis for the dependent story: over the plan-v2 segment's decision==block findings, recompute impact_plan from each finding's persisted verification.severity_attributes with the hard-override floor disabled; a block is "floor-driven" iff the floorless impact falls below the finding's persisted block_threshold while the floored impact met it. Report the floor-driven count/share overall and for ac_unverifiable-bearing findings specifically. Classify the ac_unverifiable!=none floor-driven findings' finding text into missing_oracle (text asserts no verification method exists) / underspecified_oracle (text demands a more specific command, file, or expected value for a check that exists) / ambiguous, classifying all of them when <=100 else a seed-42 random sample of 100, and report all three proportions. Record this as a comment on story 7d91-55fc-e07c-4101.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] A comment on this ticket records the segmented replay's full output including the segment's sidecar/ticket/finding counts AND the skipped-remainder counts by reason (different-version vs untagged), produced by the extended script.
- [ ] A comment on this ticket records the adjudication: every non-DET blocking-tier criterion mapped from current threshold to a KEEP/RAISE/LOWER/DEMOTE decision via the decision rule above, each citing its replay row signals.
- [ ] The Gerrit change containing the script skipped-counter and the "Calibration 3" section of docs/experiments/plan-review-threshold-calibration.md (plus criteria_routing.json threshold edits iff the adjudication warrants any; the no-change case is stated explicitly in that doc section) is MERGED (LLM-Review +1, Verified +1).
- [ ] A comment on story 7d91-55fc-e07c-4101 records the floor-attribution analysis: floor-driven block count/share (overall and ac_unverifiable-specific) and the missing_oracle/underspecified_oracle/ambiguous proportions with the sample-size rule stated above.
